claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit is assessed separately for each child, so I'll need information about each child you're asking about.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child (or children) you're responsible for? If yes, how old is the child (or how old are the children)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have three children - Alex is 7, Blake is 12, and Charlie is 10.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children:
- Alex (age 7)
- Blake (age 12)
- Charlie (age 10)

Let me assess each child's eligibility. Since all three children are under 16, they potentially qualify. Now I need to ask a few more questions.

**Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) working full-time for 24 hours or more per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them work at all. They're all still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, that's helpful. Now, the next question:

**Are any of your children receiving benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them receive any benefits in their own names.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to ask about your residency situation:

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since you're living abroad, there are some special circumstances that might still allow you to qualify. Let me ask:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant (for example, working for the UK government or armed forces), or are you living in a country covered by a reciprocal agreement with the UK (such as certain European countries)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad for personal reasons.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to make a determination about your Child Benefit eligibility.

Based on what you've told me:

**Unfortunately, you are NOT eligible for Child Benefit for any of your three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie).**

**The reason:** You do not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit. To be eligible, you must normally live in the UK or meet certain special circumstances, such as:
- Being a Crown servant working abroad
- Living in certain countries with reciprocal agreements
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with qualifying criteria

Since you're living abroad for personal reasons and none of these special circumstances apply, you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time, even though your children would otherwise qualify based on their ages.

If your circumstances change and you move back to the UK to live normally, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for your children at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you for explaining that to me. I understand now - since I don't live in the UK and don't have any of those special circumstances, I'm not eligible. That's disappointing, but I appreciate you taking the time to go through everything with me.
